creator: mkhitrov@gmail.com
text:
I just updated everything on my system, including all the ports,
kernel, and base OS. It's now running 7.0-BETA4. Unfortunately the
problem persists. I think at this point it may be worth updating the
xfce ports to the 4.4.2 to see if, like Thomas said, that resolves the
problem. I don't have any other FreeBSD workstations to test this
with, and can't install xorg on the servers.

The only setting in make.conf that could have affected compilation for
me is CPUTYPE=core2, everything else is on default values. I tried
rebuilding the panel without that and nothing changed. Actually, now
that I think about it, xfce was running fine on my laptop (6.2) a few
months ago before I switched it to gnome. Don't remember what version
of xfce that was though.

- Max
